title: Move Semantics 3
description: Instead of transferring ownership, you can borrow a value using a reference (&). This lets the original owner keep the value.
instructions: Fix the code so that both vec0 and vec1 can be used by using borrowing instead of moving.
hints: The problem is that fill_vec takes ownership of vec0.
Change fill_vec to take a reference `&Vec<i32>` instead.
Create a new vector inside fill_vec by cloning the reference, then push to it.
explanation: By taking a reference (`&Vec<i32>`) instead of the value itself, the function borrows the vector without taking ownership. The original `vec0` remains valid after the function call. Inside the function, we clone the borrowed vector to create a new one we can modify.
